meta.deckTitle: Kpow for Confluent Cloud — TD Bank

slides.heading: Kpow for Confluent Cloud
slides.body: How our platform team gives every Kafka stakeholder — SRE, ops, and application teams — one console for Confluent Cloud.

slides.heading: Confluent Cloud is powerful. Visibility isn't included.
bullets: Confluent Cloud gives us a managed, resilient Kafka backbone — but day-to-day questions ("why is this consumer lagging?", "who touched this topic?") still need tooling on top.
Without a shared console, every team builds its own scripts against the Admin API — inconsistent, unaudited, and slow to onboard.
Kpow sits on top of Confluent Cloud as a single pane of glass for observability, operations, and governed self-service.
slides.imageAlt: Kpow dashboard showing real-time cluster health, consumer lag, and topic inspection
slides.imageCaption: Kpow's home view: cluster health, consumer lag, and topic activity in one screen.

slides.heading: Observe: real-time health, down to the partition
bullets: Broker sync state and under-replicated partitions surfaced immediately — no more waiting on a Grafana lag alert.
Consumer lag broken down by group, topic, and partition, so on-call can pinpoint the exact bottleneck.
Kafka Streams topologies rendered live with per-node metrics, and everything exportable to our existing Prometheus stack.
slides.imageAlt: Kpow Observe view with cluster health and consumer lag metrics
slides.imageCaption: Observe: cluster health and consumer lag, broken down by partition.

slides.heading: Manage & Inspect: safe changes, fast troubleshooting
bullets: Topic, ACL, consumer group, schema, and connector lifecycle — all from one console, every action captured in an audit trail.
kJQ lets us search across hundreds of topics in seconds, filtering by key, header, or message content, with schema-aware decoding for Avro/Protobuf.
What used to be a 4-hour, multi-tool incident chase becomes a single search — no more tailing logs blind.
slides.imageAlt: Kpow Manage view showing topic and consumer group operations
slides.imageCaption: Manage: topic, consumer group, and connector operations with a full audit trail.

slides.heading: Govern: self-service, without losing control
bullets: Role-based access, scoped per team and per environment, so application teams get read/write where they need it — and nowhere else.
SSO through our existing Okta/Azure AD, so onboarding a new team is a config change, not a ticket queue.
Field-level PII masking keeps message payloads safe to inspect, and every action — UI, webhook, or API — is logged for audit.
This is how we replaced "file a platform ticket and wait" with governed self-service for our internal developer teams.
slides.imageAlt: Kpow Govern view showing role-based access control and audit logs
slides.imageCaption: Govern: role-based access, SSO, PII masking, and audit logging.

slides.heading: We're not the first bank to bet on this

quote.text: Thanks to Kpow, we halved the time needed for debugging. When it's bad data, we're very effective at figuring out why it's bad.
quote.attribution: Erik Schumann, Central Kafka Team, NORD/LB
